message:
fix: propagate context.Context through store layer and benchmark handlers (#9736)

* fix: propagate context.Context through store layer and benchmark handlers

Thread ctx context.Context through all Store interface methods and their
SQLiteStore implementations so request cancellation (client disconnect,
timeout) aborts in-flight SQL queries via QueryContext/ExecContext.

Replace context.Background() in BenchmarkHandlers.GetReports and
StreamReports with the request context (c.UserContext()) so Google Drive
API calls and child goroutines stop when the client navigates away.

Fixes #9729, Fixes #9732

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: eliminate websocket closeConn / releaseConn data race (#9736)

closeConn() called conn.Close() on the gofiber websocket.Conn wrapper,
which raced with the middleware's deferred releaseConn() setting the
embedded *websocket.Conn field to nil. The race detector flagged the
concurrent read (Close) and write (nil assignment) on the same pointer.

Fix: capture the underlying net.Conn at Client creation time and close
that instead. This avoids touching the gofiber wrapper entirely while
still triggering ReadMessage errors that cause the handler to exit
cleanly. Tests updated to capture net.Conn inside the handler callback
where it is safe from releaseConn.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: update ts-null-safety ratchet baselines to match current main counts

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -501,7 +501,7 @@ func TestOAuthStatePersistence_RoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
 	h, _ := newRealStoreAuthHandler(t)
 
 	const state = "round-trip-state"
-	require.NoError(t, h.storeOAuthState(state))
+	require.NoError(t, h.storeOAuthState(context.Background(), state))
 
 	ok := h.validateAndConsumeOAuthState(context.Background(), state)
 	assert.True(t, ok, "freshly stored state should validate")
@@ -530,7 +530,7 @@ func TestOAuthStatePersistence_SurvivesRestart(t *testing.T) {
 		BackendURL:     "http://backend",
 	})
 	const state = "state-across-restart"
-	require.NoError(t, h1.storeOAuthState(state))
+	require.NoError(t, h1.storeOAuthState(context.Background(), state))
 	require.NoError(t, s1.Close())
 
 	// Second "process" — /auth/callback consumes the state after restart.

@@ -517,7 +517,7 @@ func (h *BenchmarkHandlers) GetReports(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
 	}
 
 	// Fetch from Google Drive
-	reports, parseFailures, err := h.fetchAllReports(context.Background(), cutoff)
+	reports, parseFailures, err := h.fetchAllReports(c.UserContext(), cutoff)
 	if err != nil {
 		slog.Error("[benchmarks] Google Drive fetch error", "error", err)
 		h.cache.mu.RLock()
@@ -580,10 +580,15 @@ func (h *BenchmarkHandlers) StreamReports(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
 	c.Set("Cache-Control", "no-cache")
 	c.Set("Connection", "keep-alive")
 
+	// Capture the request context before entering the stream writer callback
+	// so cancellation propagates from the HTTP request to all Drive API calls (#9732).
+	reqCtx := c.UserContext()
+
 	c.Context().SetBodyStreamWriter(func(w *bufio.Writer) {
-		// Create a cancellable context so all goroutines and Drive API calls
-		// abort promptly when the client disconnects (#9517).
-		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+		// Create a cancellable context derived from the request context so all
+		// goroutines and Drive API calls abort promptly when the client
+		// disconnects (#9517, #9732).
+		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(reqCtx)
 		defer cancel()
 
 		allReports := make([]BenchmarkReport, 0)

 func TestHubSlowClientDisconnect(t *testing.T) {
 	// 2b. Test that a slow client (buffer full) disconnects without blocking the Run loop
 	var serverConn *websocket.Conn
+	var serverNetConn net.Conn // #9736 — capture inside handler to avoid releaseConn race
 	var connReady sync.WaitGroup
 	connReady.Add(1)
 
 	_, wsURL := setupWSServer(t, func(c *websocket.Conn) {
 		serverConn = c
+		serverNetConn = c.NetConn()
 		connReady.Done()
 	})
